data.title: DOGE Treasury Company Seeks $200 Million Investment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a proposal for raising funds has been circulated among investors by a DOGE treasury company, aiming to invest specifically in DOGE. Six anonymous sources have confirmed this, with three directly involved in the project presentation and the other three informed by related parties.

Two sources indicated that the company intends to raise at least $200 million. However, details regarding the structure of the listed entity and its launch timeline remain undisclosed.

Three sources revealed that the official DOGE entity, "House of Doge," has signed an agreement to approve this plan, establishing itself as the "official" treasury operation platform for DOGE. This company was founded by the DOGE Foundation in early 2025 and is headquartered in Miami, focusing on promoting and developing the DOGE token.

Fundraising documents sent to potential investors and information from five insiders suggest that Elon Musk's personal lawyer, Alex Spiro, is expected to become the chairman of this listed company.

data.title: Flare Partners with Everything Blockchain Inc. to Enhance Enterprise Bond Yields
data.description:
According to PANews, blockchain company Flare has signed a memorandum of understanding with U.S.-listed firm Everything Blockchain Inc. (OTC: EBZT) to utilize its XRP finance (XRPFi) framework for enterprise bond yields. Central to this framework is Flare's "FAssets" system, a trustless bridge that provides smart contract capabilities for tokens like XRP and Bitcoin. By integrating Flare's re-collateralization layer, Firelight, the system enables enterprises to convert XRP into FXRP and allocate it across decentralized lending, collateral, and liquidity protocols.

Several months ago, Nasdaq-listed VivoPower International PLC (NASDAQ: VVPR) committed $100 million in XRP to the Flare ecosystem, making EBZT the second publicly traded company to do so.

data.title: Eliza Labs Files Antitrust Lawsuit Against Social Media Platform
data.description: According to PANews, Eliza Labs and its founder, Shaw Walters, have initiated a federal antitrust lawsuit against social media platform X on August 27. The lawsuit alleges that the platform fraudulently acquired technical information from Eliza Labs before removing its AI agent and launching a competing product. The plaintiffs are seeking damages exceeding $75,000 and the immediate reinstatement of their account. Walters stated on August 28 that the lawsuit was a last resort following months of unsuccessful negotiations.
